For what value(s) of \( x \) does the equality \( (x+1)(x-2) = x+1 \) hold?
A. \( -1 \) only
B. \( 2 \) only
C. Any value
D. Any value except \( -1 \)
E. Any value except \( 2 \)

Which of the following is a factor of \( 4(a+b)^2 - 9(a-b)^2 \)?
A. \( 5b-a \)
B. \( 5a+b \)
C. \( -a-b \)
D. \( 13b-5a \)
E. \( 13a-5b \)

If \( \dfrac{a}{b} = \dfrac{c}{d} = k \) and \( a, b, c, d \) are positive, then which of the following must be true?
A. \( \dfrac{a+c}{b+d} = k \)
B. \( \dfrac{a}{b} = \dfrac{c}{d} = k \)
C. \( ac = bd = k \)
D. \( a = c = k \)
E. \( ac = kb \)

Simplify \( n \times n \times n \times \dots \times n \) (\( n \) times) \( \div (n + n + \dots + n) \) (\( n \) terms).
A. \( n^{n-2} \)
B. \( n^{n-1} \)
C. \( n^{-2} \)
D. \( n^{-1} \)
E. \( 1 \)

If \( a \) and \( b \) are greater than \( 1 \), which of the following statements is/are true?
I. \( \sqrt{a+b} = \sqrt{a} + \sqrt{b} \)
II. \( a^{-1} + b^{-1} = a+b \)
III. \( a^2 b^3 = (ab)^6 \)
A. I only
B. II only
C. III only
D. I and II only
E. None of them

Suppose \( x \) varies directly as \( y^2 \) and inversely as \( z \). Find the percentage increase of \( x \) when \( y \) is increased by \( 20\% \) and \( z \) is decreased by \( 20\% \).
A. \( 15.2\% \)
B. \( 20\% \)
C. \( 50\% \)
D. \( 72.8\% \)
E. \( 80\% \)

A sum of \( \$10\,000 \) is deposited at \( 4\% \) p.a. compounded yearly. Find the interest earned in the second year.
A. \( \$16 \)
B. \( \$400 \)
C. \( \$416 \)
D. \( \$800 \)
E. \( \$816 \)

The figure shows a solid platform with steps on one side and a slope on the other. Find its volume.

A. \( 0.75 \, \text{m}^3 \)
B. \( 0.84 \, \text{m}^3 \)
C. \( 0.858 \, \text{m}^3 \)
D. \( 1.008 \, \text{m}^3 \)
E. \( 1.608 \, \text{m}^3 \)

In the figure, \( TP \) and \( TQ \) are tangents to the circle of radius \( 3 \, \text{cm} \). Find the length of the minor arc \( PQ \).

A. \( 3 \, \text{cm} \)
B. \( 2\pi \, \text{cm} \)
C. \( \dfrac{3\pi}{2} \, \text{cm} \)
D. \( \pi \, \text{cm} \)
E. \( \dfrac{\pi}{2} \, \text{cm} \)

Find the ratio of the volume of the tetrahedron \( ACHD \) to the volume of the cube \( ABCDEFGH \) in the figure.

A. \( 1:8 \)
B. \( 1:6 \)
C. \( 1:4 \)
D. \( 1:3 \)
E. \( 1:2 \)

In the figure, the equilateral triangle \( ACE \) of side \( 4 \, \text{cm} \) is inscribed in the circle. Find the area of the inscribed regular hexagon \( AFBCDE \).

A. \( 8\sqrt{3} \, \text{cm}^2 \)
B. \( 8\sqrt{2} \, \text{cm}^2 \)
C. \( 4\sqrt{3} \, \text{cm}^2 \)
D. \( 4\sqrt{2} \, \text{cm}^2 \)
E. \( 16 \, \text{cm}^2 \)

In the figure, a cone of height \( 3h \) is cut by a plane parallel to its base into a smaller cone of height \( h \) and a frustum. Find the ratio of the volume of the smaller cone to the volume of the frustum.

A. \( 1:27 \)
B. \( 1:26 \)
C. \( 1:9 \)
D. \( 1:8 \)
E. \( 1:7 \)

The greatest value of \( 1 - 2\sin^2\theta \) is
A. \( 5 \)
B. \( 3 \)
C. \( 1 \)
D. \( 0 \)
E. \( -1 \)

In the figure, find \( \cos\theta \).

A. \( \dfrac{1}{4} \)
B. \( \dfrac{1}{3} \)
C. \( \dfrac{1}{2} \)
D. \( \dfrac{2}{3} \)
E. \( \dfrac{3}{4} \)

In which two quadrants will the solution(s) of \( \sin\theta \cos\theta < 0 \) lie?
A. In quadrants I and II only
B. In quadrants I and III only
C. In quadrants II and IV only
D. In quadrants II and IV only
E. In quadrants III and IV only

If \( A+B+C = 180^\circ \), then \( 1 + \cos A \cos(B+C) = \)
A. \( 0 \)
B. \( \sin^2 A \)
C. \( 1 + \cos^2 A \)
D. \( 1 + \sin A \cos A \)
E. \( 1 - \sin A \cos A \)

The figure shows the graph of the function \( y = \)

A. \( \tan(x+\pi) \)
B. \( \tan(x-\pi) \)
C. \( \pi \tan x \)
D. \( \pi + \tan x \)
E. \( \pi - \tan x \)

Which of the following equations has/haves solutions?
I. \( 2\cos^2\theta - \sin^2 2\theta = 1 \)
II. \( 2\cos^2\theta - \sin^2\theta = 2 \)
III. \( 2\cos^2\theta - \sin^2\theta = 3 \)
A. I only
B. II only
C. III only
D. I and II only
E. II and III only

A. \( 42^\circ \)
B. \( 36^\circ \)
C. \( 24^\circ \)
D. \( 21^\circ \)
E. \( 18^\circ \)

A. \( \sqrt{6} \)
B. \( \sqrt{5} \)
C. \( \sqrt{10} \)
D. \( 6\sqrt{3} \)
E. \( 2\sqrt{5} \)

A. \( 30^\circ \)
B. \( 32^\circ \)
C. \( 35^\circ \)
D. \( 36^\circ \)
E. \( 45^\circ \)

In the figure, \( ST \) is a tangent to the smaller circle. \( ABC \) is a straight line. If \( \angle TAD = 2x \) and \( \angle DPC = 3x \), find \( x \).

A. \( 30^\circ \)
B. \( 36^\circ \)
C. \( 40^\circ \)
D. \( 42^\circ \)
E. \( 45^\circ \)

If the two lines \( 2x-y+1=0 \) and \( ax+3y-1=0 \) do not intersect, then \( a = \)
A. \( -6 \)
B. \( -2 \)
C. \( 2 \)
D. \( 3 \)
E. \( 6 \)

If \( 0 < k < h \), which of the following circles intersect(s) the \( y \)-axis?
I. \( (x-h)^2 + (y-k)^2 = 2 \)
II. \( (x-h)^2 + (y-k)^2 = h^2 \)
III. \( (x-h)^2 + (y-k)^2 = h^2+k^2 \)
A. I only
B. II only
C. III only
D. I and II only
E. II and III only

If the line \( y=mx+3 \) divides the circle \( x^2+y^2-4x-2y-5=0 \) into two equal parts, find \( m \).
A. \( \dfrac{1}{4} \)
B. \( -1 \)
C. \( 0 \)
D. \( \dfrac{5}{4} \)
E. \( 2 \)

The mid-points of the sides of a triangle are \( (3, 4) \), \( (2, 0) \) and \( (4, 2) \). Which of the following points is a vertex of the triangle?
A. \( (3.5, 3) \)
B. \( (3, 2) \)
C. \( (3, 1) \)
D. \( (1.5, 2) \)
E. \( (1, 2) \)

The table shows the mean marks of two classes of students in a mathematics test.
| Class | Number of students | Mean mark |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Class A | 38 | 72 |
| Class B | 42 | 54 |
A student in Class A has scored \( 91 \) marks. It is found that his score was wrongly recorded as \( 19 \) in the calculation of the mean mark for Class A in the above table. Find the correct mean mark of the \( 80 \) students in the two classes.
A. \( 61.65 \)
B. \( 62.55 \)
C. \( 63 \)
D. \( 63.45 \)
E. \( 63.9 \)

Two cards are drawn randomly from five cards A, B, C, D and E. Find the probability that card A is drawn while card C is not.
A. \( \dfrac{3}{25} \)
B. \( \dfrac{2}{5} \)
C. \( \dfrac{4}{25} \)
D. \( \dfrac{6}{25} \)
E. \( \dfrac{3}{20} \)

The figure shows the cumulative frequency curves of three distributions. Arrange the three distributions in the order of their standard deviation.

A. I < II < III
B. I < III < II
C. II < I < III
D. II < III < I
E. III < II < I

If the quadratic equation \( ax^2-2bx+c=0 \) has two equal roots, which of the following is/are true?
I. \( a, b, c \) form an arithmetic sequence.
II. \( a, b, c \) form a geometric sequence.
III. Both roots are \( \dfrac{b}{a} \).
A. I only
B. II only
C. III only
D. I and II only
E. II and III only

Which of the following intervals must contain a root of \( 2x^3-x^2-x-3=0 \)?
I. \( -1 < x < 1 \)
II. \( 0 < x < 2 \)
III. \( 1 < x < 3 \)
A. I only
B. II only
C. III only
D. I and II only
E. II and III only

How many integers \( x \) satisfy the inequality \( 6x^2-7x-20 \le 0 \)?
A. \( 0 \)
B. \( 1 \)
C. \( 2 \)
D. \( 3 \)
E. \( 4 \)

From the figure, if \( \alpha \le x \le \beta \), then

A. \( ax^2 + (b-m)x + (c-k) \le 0 \)
B. \( ax^2 + (b-m)x + (c-k) < 0 \)
C. \( ax^2 + (b-m)x + (c-k) = 0 \)
D. \( ax^2 + (b-m)x + (c-k) > 0 \)
E. \( ax^2 + (b-m)x + (c-k) \ge 0 \)

Under which of the following conditions must the mean of \( n \) consecutive positive integers also be an integer?
A. \( n \) is any positive integer
B. \( n \) is any positive odd integer
C. \( n \) is any positive even integer
D. \( n \) is any multiple of \( 3 \)
E. \( n \) is the square of any positive integer

The L.C.M. of \( P \) and \( Q \) is \( 12a^3b^3c^2 \). The L.C.M. of \( X, Y \) and \( Z \) is \( 30a^2b^3c \). What is the L.C.M. of \( P, Q, X, Y \) and \( Z \)?
A. \( 360a^3b^3c^2 \)
B. \( 60a^3b^3c^2 \)
C. \( 60ab^3c^2 \)
D. \( 6a^2b^3c \)
E. \( 6ab^3c \)

If a polynomial \( f(x) \) is divisible by \( x-1 \), then \( f(x-1) \) is divisible by
A. \( x-2 \)
B. \( x+2 \)
C. \( x-1 \)
D. \( x+1 \)
E. \( x \)

If the price of an orange rises by \( \$1 \), then \( 5 \) fewer oranges could be bought for \( \$100 \). Which of the following equations gives the original price \( \$x \) of an orange?
A. \( \dfrac{100}{x+1} = \dfrac{100}{x} + 5 \)
B. \( \dfrac{100}{x} - \dfrac{100}{x+1} = 5 \)
C. \( \dfrac{100}{x-1} - \dfrac{100}{x} = 5 \)
D. \( \dfrac{100}{x} - \dfrac{100}{x-1} = 5 \)
E. \( \dfrac{100}{x} + \dfrac{100}{x+1} = 5 \)

By selling an article at \( 10\% \) discount off the marked price, a shop still makes \( 20\% \) profit. If the cost price of the article is \( \$19\,800 \), then the marked price is
A. \( \$21\,600 \)
B. \( \$26\,136 \)
C. \( \$26\,400 \)
D. \( \$27\,225 \)
E. \( \$27\,500 \)

Coffee A and coffee B are mixed in the ratio \( x:y \) by weight. A costs \( \$50/\text{kg} \) and B costs \( \$40/\text{kg} \). If the cost of A is increased by \( 10\% \) while that of B is decreased by \( 15\% \), the cost of the mixture per kg remains unchanged. Find \( x:y \).
A. \( 2:3 \)
B. \( 5:6 \)
C. \( 6:5 \)
D. \( 3:2 \)
E. \( 55:34 \)
